text: Writing clean code is an essential skill for any programmer. The main goal is to make your code as readable as possible, not just for the computer, but for other humans—including your future self. One of the easiest ways to achieve this is by following clear naming conventions. Variables and functions should have names that describe their purpose, such as "calculateTotalPrice" instead of "ctp". Additionally, it is good practice to break your program into small, focused functions that each do one thing well. This improves maintainability, as you can easily find, understand, and modify specific parts of the code without breaking everything else. Clean code saves time and reduces frustration in the long run.
